info.name: HiLite Sales OS API
info.description: Multi-tenant Sales ERP API — five modules, two auth principals. See API.md in the repo for the full narrative. Run "Platform Login" first, then "Create Organization" (saves org code + temp password), then "Org Login" — most other requests build on those collection variables automatically via each request's Tests script.

request.description: Creates the org + its first admin user in one transaction. enabledModuleKeys is omittable (defaults to every current module).
Lockout recovery — new temp password, kills the admin's sessions, re-flags must_change_password.
status: "active" or "suspended". Suspending revokes every session for every user in that org.
Upsert — moduleId is the numeric id from the module catalog (see Modules Catalog > List Modules).
key is immutable once set: lowercase letters/numbers/underscores only.
key cannot be changed here.
Use the org admin's email with {{adminTempPassword}} from Create Organization (or the org's reset-admin-password response).
Requires the CURRENT password — use this for a fresh temp password's forced first change.
Members become unassigned (team_id = null), not deleted.
dataScope: own / team / organization. Defaults to "own" if omitted.
Every org is seeded with 4 defaults: Executive (own), Team Lead (team), Sales Manager (team), Director (organization).
Works on default roles too — rename and/or rescope freely.
400 if it's a default role; 409 if any user still holds it.
teamId/roleId are independently re-verified as belonging to this org. Returns a one-time tempPassword.
Lockout recovery — new temp password, kills the user's sessions, re-flags must_change_password.
Deactivating revokes that user's sessions immediately. The org's own admin account can't be deactivated this way.
Read-only — there is no write route on this side. Enabling a module for this org is exclusively a platform-admin action (see Module 1 > Organizations > Update Organization Modules).

item.name: Leads (any org user, scoped by role)

request.description: Defaults to self-assignment if assignedUserId is omitted. Publishes lead.created + lead.assigned events.
Pre-filtered by the caller's data_scope before any of these query filters apply.
Candidates the caller could assign a lead to — scoped the same way as the leads list itself.
Every field optional. Same access check as viewing — NOT the assignment check. Excludes status/assignedUserId on purpose.
One of: new, contacted, visit_scheduled, site_visit_completed, negotiation, won, lost. Publishes lead.won when set to "won".
Manual target is re-verified as within the caller's scope.
Bonus auto-assignment — picks whichever eligible active user (within the caller's scope) has the fewest open leads.
type: phone_call / meeting / site_visit / virtual_meeting.
Always includes "personal". Additionally includes "team" (data_scope=team) or "organization" (data_scope=organization, or any admin) — check the "scope" field in the response to see which.
404 if the notification doesn't belong to the caller.
